Sexually Transmitted Infections Treatment Guidelines, 2021

Granuloma Inguinale (Donovanosis) 
Print 
Minus 
Related Pages 
Granuloma inguinale (donovanosis) is a genital ulcerative disease caused by the intracellular gram-negative bacterium Klebsiella granulomatis (formerly known as Calymmatobacterium granulomatis ). The disease occurs rarely in the United States; however, sporadic cases have been described in India, South Africa, and South America ( 526 – 535 ). Although granuloma inguinale was previously endemic in Australia, it is now extremely rare ( 536 , 537 ). Clinically, the disease is characterized as painless, slowly progressive ulcerative lesions on the genitals or perineum w
